perturbation.original_text: Distributor has the right to cancel, reschedule or modify all or any portion of a purchase order that has been accepted by Xplore at no cost to Distributor only if such cancellation or modification request is made within [*****] business days of PO acceptance or rescheduling request is made at least [*****] business days prior to the most current Estimated Shipping Date for that purchase order.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor has the right to cancel, reschedule or modify all or any portion of a purchase order that has been accepted by Xplore at no cost to Distributor. Distributor can cancel or modify all or any portion of a purchase order within 2 business days of PO acceptance or rescheduling request made at least 2 business days prior to the most current Estimated Shipping Date for that purchase order. Distributor has the right to change the destination of all or any portion of a purchase order that has been accepted by Xplore at no cost to Distributor only if such change in destination request is made at least [*****] business days prior to the most current Estimated Shipping Date for that purchase order.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction by explicitly stating that the distributor can cancel, reschedule, or modify purchase orders without mentioning any restriction period. It contradicts the very next sentence that implies a time restriction in which Distributor can cancel or modify all or any portion of a purchase order within 2 business days of PO acceptance, making the time frame for the modification non-existant, and ambiguous. This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the allowed time frame for cancellation or modification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Purchase Order Rescheduling, Cancellation and Modification. Distributor has the right to cancel, reschedule or modify all or any portion of a purchase order that has been accepted by Xplore at no cost to Distributor. Distributor can cancel or modify all or any portion of a purchase order within 2 business days of PO acceptance or rescheduling request made at least 2 business days prior to the most current Estimated Shipping Date for that purchase order. Distributor has the right to change the destination of all or any portion of a purchase order that has been accepted by Xplore at no cost to Distributor only if such change in destination request is made at least [*****] business days prior to the most current Estimated Shipping Date for that purchase order. Except as set forth above, purchase orders are non-changeable and non- cancellable by Distributor, once accepted by Xplore. Certain purchase orders, determined at Xplore's sole discretion, and generally of large volume and/or extended lead times, may be subject to alternative rescheduling, cancellation, and modification rights. Should such purchase orders be subject to alternative rights, Xplore shall inform Distributor of alternative rights prior to order acceptance. Distributor will then have [*****] business days to accept, or reject, the alternative terms of Xplore for that certain purchase order. If Distributor rejects such alternative terms, the purchase order will not be accepted by Xplore.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that the distributor can cancel, reschedule, or modify purchase orders without any specified restriction period. This conflicts with the subsequent sentence, which sets a 2-business day limit for cancellations or modifications after PO acceptance and a 2-business day requirement before the Estimated Shipping Date for rescheduling. The 'Contradicted Text' section contains the conflicting statements, making the allowable timeframe for modification unclear and inconsistent, which makes this a direct contradiction that could create legal issues regarding the modification terms.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain thirty (30) days of stock in Distributor's inventory to support sales. Xplore acknowledges that from time to time, Distributor's inventory levels may fall below the thirty (30) days goal that is agreed upon by both Parties. If inventory levels fall below the thirty (30) day goal for more than sixty (60) consecutive days, Xplore, upon written notice to Distributor, shall replenish the stock to an amount agreed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor shall maintain thirty (30) days of stock in Distributor's inventory to support sales. Xplore acknowledges that from time to time, Distributor's inventory levels may fall below the thirty (30) days goal that is agreed upon by both Parties. If inventory levels fall below the thirty (30) day goal for more than sixty (60) consecutive days, Xplore, upon written notice to Distributor, shall replenish the stock to an amount agreed by both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The change removes the 'commercially reasonable efforts' qualifier, making the 30-day stock maintenance a strict obligation. This directly contradicts the following sentence, which acknowledges that inventory levels may fall below the 30-day target, effectively creating ambiguity about the enforceability of the 30-day stock maintenance requirement. One sentence asserts a strict requirement, while the other acknowledges its potential non-compliance, leading to uncertainty. The use of the word 'shall' in the first sentence means Distributor is committed in doing so and can be penalized for it, where as the very next statement implies that inventory levels may fall below the thirty days goal which makes it contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Stock on Hand. Distributor shall maintain thirty (30) days of stock in Distributor's inventory to support sales. Xplore acknowledges that from time to time, Distributor's inventory levels may fall below the thirty (30) days goal that is agreed upon by both Parties. If inventory levels fall below the thirty (30) day goal for more than sixty (60) consecutive days, Xplore, upon written notice to Distributor, shall replenish the stock to an amount agreed by both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for 'commercially reasonable efforts' to maintain the 30-day inventory, which provided flexibility. The changed text removes this qualifier, imposing a strict obligation to maintain 30 days of stock. This creates a direct conflict with the subsequent sentence, which acknowledges that inventory levels may fall below the 30-day target. The term 'shall' indicates a mandatory action, leading to a strong contradiction when juxtaposed with the acknowledgment of potential non-compliance, making the obligation ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The terms of Section 3 of Schedule 2 of the Distribution Agreement will apply to Xplore Products, provided however that stock rotation allowance for Xplore Products will be based on the net dollar value of Distributor's purchases in each calendar quarter of Xplore Products and such allowance will be calculated separate and apart from all other Products purchased by Distributor during such period.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms of Section 3 of Schedule 2 of the Distribution Agreement will apply to Xplore Products, except that there will be no stock rotation allowance for Xplore Products.
perturbation.explanation: The modification creates a direct contradiction by first stating that the stock rotation terms of Section 3 of Schedule 2 apply, then immediately negating that application by stating there's no stock rotation allowance for Xplore products. This causes ambiguity, as it's unclear whether Section 3 applies at all or if the original terms are overruled. The word 'except' directly contradicts the first part of the statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Product Return and Stock Rotation. The terms of Section 3 of Schedule 2 of the Distribution Agreement will apply to Xplore Products, except that there will be no stock rotation allowance for Xplore Products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states 'except that there will be no stock rotation allowance for Xplore Products.' This directly contradicts the initial statement in both the changed text and the contradicted text, which says that 'The terms of Section 3 of Schedule 2 of the Distribution Agreement will apply to Xplore Products'. Section 3 apparently outlines stock rotation allowances, so eliminating this allowance creates a direct inconsistency and renders the application of Section 3 to Xplore Products unclear and contradictory. This fulfills the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition by providing conflicting obligations regarding stock rotation.
